---
title: Solver Reference
---

> Auto-generated 2026-05-06 10:31 UTC &nbsp;·&nbsp; 18 solvers &nbsp;·&nbsp; 3 physics domains &nbsp;·&nbsp; 80 differentiable fields

Each solver card shows its numerical scheme, AD strategy, known limitations, and Tesseract schema. Click **Inputs / Outputs** on any solver to expand its field tables. The ∂ column marks fields that support automatic differentiation (VJP/JVP).

## Navier–Stokes (Grid)

### Exponax

**Scheme:** spectral ETDRK &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** jax &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Reverse-mode AD &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float32 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** Yes

**Upstream docs:** [https://fkoehler.site/exponax/](https://fkoehler.site/exponax/)

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/navier-stokes-grid/exponax`

Integrates the 3D Navier–Stokes equations with an exponential time-differencing Runge–Kutta (ETDRK) spectral scheme, enforcing incompressibility to machine precision by construction. Gradients via JAX source-transformation AD.

**Wrapping:** Pure JAX: Exponax is an Equinox-based JAX library. The solver is called as a traced JAX function; VJP via ``jax.vjp``.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `v0` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Initial velocity field, shape (N, N, 1, 2). Default: 64×64 divergence-free random vortex field (seed=42). |
| `viscosity` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Kinematic viscosity ν. |
| `dt` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Timestep size. |
| `steps` | `int` |  | Number of timesteps. Total simulated time = steps × dt. |
| `domain_extent` | `float` |  | Side length of the square periodic domain. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `GridBC` |  | Per-face boundary conditions. Default: all periodic. |
| `obstacle` | `GridObstacle | None` |  | Optional solid obstacle embedded in the domain (no-slip walls). |
| `inflow_profile` | `Differentiable[Array[(None,), Float32]] | None` |  | 1-D inlet velocity profile u_x(y), shape (N,). When provided, overrides the x_lo Dirichlet BC with a spatially-varying inlet. u_y is set to zero. Used for inflow-profile optimisation (e.g. cylinder drag minimisation). |
| `lid_velocity` | `Differentiable[Array[(None, None, 2), Float32]] | None` |  | Lid velocity field for 3-D lid-driven cavity experiments, shape (N, N, 2). The two components are the x- and y-tangential velocities on the moving top lid. When provided the solver runs in non-periodic cavity mode with no-slip walls. Used as the optimisation variable in lid_cavity experiments. |
| `drag` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Linear drag coefficient |
| `order` | `int` |  | ETDRK time integration order (0-4) |
| `kolmogorov_forcing` | `bool` |  | Enable Kolmogorov sinusoidal body forcing for sustained turbulence |
| `injection_mode` | `int` |  | Wavenumber at which energy is injected (Kolmogorov forcing) |
| `injection_scale` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Amplitude of the Kolmogorov body forcing |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `result` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Final velocity field, same shape as v0. |
| `drag` | `Differentiable[Array[(1,), Float32]] | None` |  | x-direction drag force on the embedded obstacle, shape (1,). Computed as the surface integral of pressure and viscous stress: F_x = ∮_S (p n_x − μ (∂u/∂n)_x) dS. None when no obstacle is present. |
| `velocity_mean` | `Differentiable[GridVectorField] | None` |  | Time-averaged (RANS) velocity field over the last steps // 2 steps of the rollout, same shape as result. Computed on solvers feasible for drag optimisation (xlb, phiflow, pict) when an obstacle and inflow profile are present. None on periodic/lid-cavity paths. |

:::

### INS.jl

**Scheme:** FD + projection &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** julia &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Reverse-mode AD &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float64 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** No

**Upstream docs:** [https://agdestein.github.io/IncompressibleNavierStokes.jl/dev/](https://agdestein.github.io/IncompressibleNavierStokes.jl/dev/)

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/navier-stokes-grid/incompressible-navier-stokes-jl`

IncompressibleNavierStokes.jl: Julia finite-difference pressure-projection solver. Differentiates through the time loop via Zygote.jl reverse-mode AD. CPU only.

**Wrapping:** Julia subprocess: IncompressibleNavierStokes.jl is called via ``juliacall``. VJP via Zygote.jl reverse-mode AD through the time loop.

::: {.callout-warning}
#### Known limitations

- Viscosity gradient uses finite differences (Zygote returns NoTangent for the diffusion operator). FD gradients diverge as rollout length grows.
- Brinkman penalization incompatible with spectral pressure solve. Excluded from drag optimization.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `v0` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Initial velocity field, shape (N, N, 1, 2). Default: 64×64 divergence-free random vortex field (seed=42). |
| `viscosity` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Kinematic viscosity ν. |
| `dt` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Timestep size. |
| `steps` | `int` |  | Number of timesteps. Total simulated time = steps × dt. |
| `domain_extent` | `float` |  | Side length of the square periodic domain. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `GridBC` |  | Per-face boundary conditions. Default: all periodic. |
| `obstacle` | `GridObstacle | None` |  | Optional solid obstacle embedded in the domain (no-slip walls). |
| `inflow_profile` | `Differentiable[Array[(None,), Float32]] | None` |  | 1-D inlet velocity profile u_x(y), shape (N,). When provided, overrides the x_lo Dirichlet BC with a spatially-varying inlet. u_y is set to zero. Used for inflow-profile optimisation (e.g. cylinder drag minimisation). |
| `lid_velocity` | `Differentiable[Array[(None, None, 2), Float32]] | None` |  | Lid velocity field for 3-D lid-driven cavity experiments, shape (N, N, 2). The two components are the x- and y-tangential velocities on the moving top lid. When provided the solver runs in non-periodic cavity mode with no-slip walls. Used as the optimisation variable in lid_cavity experiments. |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `result` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Final velocity field, same shape as v0. |
| `drag` | `Differentiable[Array[(1,), Float32]] | None` |  | x-direction drag force on the embedded obstacle, shape (1,). Computed as the surface integral of pressure and viscous stress: F_x = ∮_S (p n_x − μ (∂u/∂n)_x) dS. None when no obstacle is present. |
| `velocity_mean` | `Differentiable[GridVectorField] | None` |  | Time-averaged (RANS) velocity field over the last steps // 2 steps of the rollout, same shape as result. Computed on solvers feasible for drag optimisation (xlb, phiflow, pict) when an obstacle and inflow profile are present. None on periodic/lid-cavity paths. |

:::

### jax-cfd

**Scheme:** MAC FD + projection &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** jax &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Reverse-mode AD &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float32 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** Yes

**Upstream docs:** [https://github.com/google/jax-cfd](https://github.com/google/jax-cfd)

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/navier-stokes-grid/jax-cfd`

JAX-native incompressible flow solver on a staggered MAC grid with finite-difference advection and spectral (FFT) pressure projection. Gradients via JAX source-transformation AD. The spectral pressure solve requires periodic BCs in all spatial directions, so JAX-CFD is included in periodic benchmarks (TGV, multimode agreement) but excluded from the cylinder-wake experiment.

**Wrapping:** Pure JAX: the solver is called as a traced JAX function inside ``apply``. VJP is obtained by wrapping the forward call with ``jax.vjp``.

::: {.callout-warning}
#### Known limitations

- Spectral pressure solve requires periodic boundary conditions. Excluded from channel/cylinder-wake experiments.
:::

::: {.callout-note}
#### Excluded experiments

- **forward/cylinder**: tesseract uses periodic FFT pressure solve + IBM volume penalization; channel-BC cylinder flow requires a non-periodic pressure solve that is not wired in this benchmark
- **recovery/drag_opt**: periodic FFT pressure solve + IBM volume penalization is incompatible with cylinder obstacle channel BCs (same root cause as forward/cylinder)
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `v0` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Initial velocity field, shape (N, N, 1, 2). Default: 64×64 divergence-free random vortex field (seed=42). |
| `viscosity` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Kinematic viscosity ν. |
| `dt` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Timestep size. |
| `steps` | `int` |  | Number of timesteps. Total simulated time = steps × dt. |
| `domain_extent` | `float` |  | Side length of the square periodic domain. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `GridBC` |  | Per-face boundary conditions. Default: all periodic. |
| `obstacle` | `GridObstacle | None` |  | Optional solid obstacle embedded in the domain (no-slip walls). |
| `inflow_profile` | `Differentiable[Array[(None,), Float32]] | None` |  | 1-D inlet velocity profile u_x(y), shape (N,). When provided, overrides the x_lo Dirichlet BC with a spatially-varying inlet. u_y is set to zero. Used for inflow-profile optimisation (e.g. cylinder drag minimisation). |
| `lid_velocity` | `Differentiable[Array[(None, None, 2), Float32]] | None` |  | Lid velocity field for 3-D lid-driven cavity experiments, shape (N, N, 2). The two components are the x- and y-tangential velocities on the moving top lid. When provided the solver runs in non-periodic cavity mode with no-slip walls. Used as the optimisation variable in lid_cavity experiments. |
| `density` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Density of the fluid |
| `inner_steps` | `int` |  | Solver sub-iterations per timestep (higher = more accurate pressure solve) |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `result` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Final velocity field, same shape as v0. |
| `drag` | `Differentiable[Array[(1,), Float32]] | None` |  | x-direction drag force on the embedded obstacle, shape (1,). Computed as the surface integral of pressure and viscous stress: F_x = ∮_S (p n_x − μ (∂u/∂n)_x) dS. None when no obstacle is present. |
| `velocity_mean` | `Differentiable[GridVectorField] | None` |  | Time-averaged (RANS) velocity field over the last steps // 2 steps of the rollout, same shape as result. Computed on solvers feasible for drag optimisation (xlb, phiflow, pict) when an obstacle and inflow profile are present. None on periodic/lid-cavity paths. |

:::

### OpenFOAM

**Scheme:** icoFoam PISO &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** cpp &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** None (forward only) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float64 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** No

**Upstream docs:** [https://www.openfoam.com/documentation/overview](https://www.openfoam.com/documentation/overview)

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/navier-stokes-grid/openfoam`

Runs the icoFoam incompressible PISO solver as a forward-only reference baseline. No reverse-mode AD available. Used as the reference solver for fluid benchmark domains.

**Wrapping:** Subprocess: icoFoam is launched as an external process. Input/output fields are serialized to/from OpenFOAM's file format. Forward only, no VJP.

::: {.callout-note}
#### Excluded experiments

- **gradient**: standard icoFoam is non-differentiable (C++, no AD path); DAFoam/OpenFOAM-AD exist but are not deployed in this tesseract
- **optimization**: standard icoFoam is non-differentiable forward-only solver
- **cost/vjp_cost**: standard icoFoam has no VJP to benchmark
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `v0` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Initial velocity field, shape (N, N, 1, 2). Default: 64×64 divergence-free random vortex field (seed=42). |
| `viscosity` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Kinematic viscosity ν. |
| `dt` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Timestep size. |
| `steps` | `int` |  | Number of timesteps. Total simulated time = steps × dt. |
| `domain_extent` | `float` |  | Side length of the square periodic domain. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `GridBC` |  | Per-face boundary conditions. Default: all periodic. |
| `obstacle` | `GridObstacle | None` |  | Optional solid obstacle embedded in the domain (no-slip walls). |
| `inflow_profile` | `Differentiable[Array[(None,), Float32]] | None` |  | 1-D inlet velocity profile u_x(y), shape (N,). When provided, overrides the x_lo Dirichlet BC with a spatially-varying inlet. u_y is set to zero. Used for inflow-profile optimisation (e.g. cylinder drag minimisation). |
| `lid_velocity` | `Differentiable[Array[(None, None, 2), Float32]] | None` |  | Lid velocity field for 3-D lid-driven cavity experiments, shape (N, N, 2). The two components are the x- and y-tangential velocities on the moving top lid. When provided the solver runs in non-periodic cavity mode with no-slip walls. Used as the optimisation variable in lid_cavity experiments. |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `result` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Final velocity field, same shape as v0. |
| `drag` | `Differentiable[Array[(1,), Float32]] | None` |  | x-direction drag force on the embedded obstacle, shape (1,). Computed as the surface integral of pressure and viscous stress: F_x = ∮_S (p n_x − μ (∂u/∂n)_x) dS. None when no obstacle is present. |
| `velocity_mean` | `Differentiable[GridVectorField] | None` |  | Time-averaged (RANS) velocity field over the last steps // 2 steps of the rollout, same shape as result. Computed on solvers feasible for drag optimisation (xlb, phiflow, pict) when an obstacle and inflow profile are present. None on periodic/lid-cavity paths. |

:::

### PhiFlow

**Scheme:** differential advection + projection &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** jax &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Reverse-mode AD &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float32 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** Yes

**Upstream docs:** [https://tum-pbs.github.io/PhiFlow/](https://tum-pbs.github.io/PhiFlow/)

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/navier-stokes-grid/phiflow`

Semi-Lagrangian advection with pressure projection. Supports PyTorch, JAX, and TensorFlow backends, enabling gradient computation through the same simulation code across AD frameworks.

**Wrapping:** Pure JAX: PhiFlow's JAX backend is selected at import time. The simulation loop runs as a traced JAX function; VJP via ``jax.vjp``.

::: {.callout-note}
#### Excluded experiments

- **cost/temporal_cost**: CUDA OOM during JAX CUDA graph profiling in 3D cost benchmark (allocate 16.09MiB failed); xla_gpu_autotune_level=0 fix deployed — pending re-run to confirm resolved
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `v0` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Initial velocity field, shape (N, N, 1, 2). Default: 64×64 divergence-free random vortex field (seed=42). |
| `viscosity` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Kinematic viscosity ν. |
| `dt` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Timestep size. |
| `steps` | `int` |  | Number of timesteps. Total simulated time = steps × dt. |
| `domain_extent` | `float` |  | Side length of the square periodic domain. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `GridBC` |  | Per-face boundary conditions. Default: all periodic. |
| `obstacle` | `GridObstacle | None` |  | Optional solid obstacle embedded in the domain (no-slip walls). |
| `inflow_profile` | `Differentiable[Array[(None,), Float32]] | None` |  | 1-D inlet velocity profile u_x(y), shape (N,). When provided, overrides the x_lo Dirichlet BC with a spatially-varying inlet. u_y is set to zero. Used for inflow-profile optimisation (e.g. cylinder drag minimisation). |
| `lid_velocity` | `Differentiable[Array[(None, None, 2), Float32]] | None` |  | Lid velocity field for 3-D lid-driven cavity experiments, shape (N, N, 2). The two components are the x- and y-tangential velocities on the moving top lid. When provided the solver runs in non-periodic cavity mode with no-slip walls. Used as the optimisation variable in lid_cavity experiments. |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `result` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Final velocity field, same shape as v0. |
| `drag` | `Differentiable[Array[(1,), Float32]] | None` |  | x-direction drag force on the embedded obstacle, shape (1,). Computed as the surface integral of pressure and viscous stress: F_x = ∮_S (p n_x − μ (∂u/∂n)_x) dS. None when no obstacle is present. |
| `velocity_mean` | `Differentiable[GridVectorField] | None` |  | Time-averaged (RANS) velocity field over the last steps // 2 steps of the rollout, same shape as result. Computed on solvers feasible for drag optimisation (xlb, phiflow, pict) when an obstacle and inflow profile are present. None on periodic/lid-cavity paths. |

:::

### PICT

**Scheme:** PISO (2nd-order) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** pytorch &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Reverse-mode AD &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float32 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** Yes

**Upstream docs:** [https://github.com/tum-pbs/PICT](https://github.com/tum-pbs/PICT)

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/navier-stokes-grid/pict`

GPU-accelerated differentiable incompressible Navier–Stokes solver built on PyTorch with custom CUDA kernels implementing the PISO algorithm. Supports reverse-mode AD through the full time loop and handles multi-block curvilinear grids. The Mosaic Tesseract exposes periodic, lid-driven cavity, inflow/channel, and cylinder-wake modes via an 8-block ring topology.

**Wrapping:** PyTorch + CUDA: PISOtorch C++ extension runs the PISO time loop on GPU. VJP via ``torch.autograd`` backward through the custom CUDA kernels.

::: {.callout-warning}
#### Known limitations

- Viscosity is not differentiable: PISOtorch_diff treats viscosity as a static scalar. Differentiation w.r.t. viscosity would require upstream changes to the C++/CUDA kernels.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `v0` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Initial velocity field, shape (N, N, 1, 2). Default: 64×64 divergence-free random vortex field (seed=42). |
| `viscosity` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Kinematic viscosity ν. |
| `dt` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Timestep size. |
| `steps` | `int` |  | Number of timesteps. Total simulated time = steps × dt. |
| `domain_extent` | `float` |  | Side length of the square periodic domain. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `GridBC` |  | Per-face boundary conditions. Default: all periodic. |
| `obstacle` | `GridObstacle | None` |  | Optional solid obstacle embedded in the domain (no-slip walls). |
| `inflow_profile` | `Differentiable[Array[(None,), Float32]] | None` |  | 1-D inlet velocity profile u_x(y), shape (N,). When provided, overrides the x_lo Dirichlet BC with a spatially-varying inlet. u_y is set to zero. Used for inflow-profile optimisation (e.g. cylinder drag minimisation). |
| `lid_velocity` | `Differentiable[Array[(None, None, 2), Float32]] | None` |  | Lid velocity field for 3-D lid-driven cavity experiments, shape (N, N, 2). The two components are the x- and y-tangential velocities on the moving top lid. When provided the solver runs in non-periodic cavity mode with no-slip walls. Used as the optimisation variable in lid_cavity experiments. |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `result` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Final velocity field, same shape as v0. |
| `drag` | `Differentiable[Array[(1,), Float32]] | None` |  | x-direction drag force on the embedded obstacle, shape (1,). Computed as the surface integral of pressure and viscous stress: F_x = ∮_S (p n_x − μ (∂u/∂n)_x) dS. None when no obstacle is present. |
| `velocity_mean` | `Differentiable[GridVectorField] | None` |  | Time-averaged (RANS) velocity field over the last steps // 2 steps of the rollout, same shape as result. Computed on solvers feasible for drag optimisation (xlb, phiflow, pict) when an obstacle and inflow profile are present. None on periodic/lid-cavity paths. |

:::

### Warp-NS

**Scheme:** IPCS (3D), wp.Tape VJP &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** warp &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Reverse-mode AD &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float32 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** Yes

**Upstream docs:** [https://github.com/NVIDIA/warp](https://github.com/NVIDIA/warp)

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/navier-stokes-grid/warp-ns`

Implements the IPCS projection scheme in NVIDIA Warp CUDA kernels with an FFT-based spectral Poisson solver. Gradients via the wp.Tape kernel-level VJP. Custom solver: the framework provides differentiable kernels but no ready-made incompressible flow solver, so FE assembly and time integration were implemented using Warp primitives. Represents the integration cost when a kernel toolkit provides no built-in solver.

**Wrapping:** NVIDIA Warp: custom CUDA kernels implement IPCS projection. VJP via ``wp.Tape`` kernel-level reverse-mode replay.

::: {.callout-warning}
#### Known limitations

- Spectral pressure solve requires periodic boundary conditions. Excluded from channel/cylinder-wake experiments.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `v0` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Initial velocity field, shape (N, N, 1, 2). Default: 64×64 divergence-free random vortex field (seed=42). |
| `viscosity` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Kinematic viscosity ν. |
| `dt` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Timestep size. |
| `steps` | `int` |  | Number of timesteps. Total simulated time = steps × dt. |
| `domain_extent` | `float` |  | Side length of the square periodic domain. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `GridBC` |  | Per-face boundary conditions. Default: all periodic. |
| `obstacle` | `GridObstacle | None` |  | Optional solid obstacle embedded in the domain (no-slip walls). |
| `inflow_profile` | `Differentiable[Array[(None,), Float32]] | None` |  | 1-D inlet velocity profile u_x(y), shape (N,). When provided, overrides the x_lo Dirichlet BC with a spatially-varying inlet. u_y is set to zero. Used for inflow-profile optimisation (e.g. cylinder drag minimisation). |
| `lid_velocity` | `Differentiable[Array[(None, None, 2), Float32]] | None` |  | Lid velocity field for 3-D lid-driven cavity experiments, shape (N, N, 2). The two components are the x- and y-tangential velocities on the moving top lid. When provided the solver runs in non-periodic cavity mode with no-slip walls. Used as the optimisation variable in lid_cavity experiments. |
| `num_iters_poisson` | `int` |  | Minimum number of Jacobi iterations per 2-D streamfunction Poisson solve. The solver auto-scales to max(this value, min(4*N², 8000)) at runtime, so convergence is maintained across grid sizes N=16..128 without manual tuning.  For N≥128 with production accuracy, a multigrid or FFT Poisson solver is recommended as Jacobi is capped at 8000 iterations. |
| `num_iters_poisson_3d` | `int` |  | Number of Jacobi iterations per 3-D pressure Poisson solve. 800 iterations is adequate for N≤32; increase for larger grids. |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `result` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Final velocity field, same shape as v0. |
| `drag` | `Differentiable[Array[(1,), Float32]] | None` |  | x-direction drag force on the embedded obstacle, shape (1,). Computed as the surface integral of pressure and viscous stress: F_x = ∮_S (p n_x − μ (∂u/∂n)_x) dS. None when no obstacle is present. |
| `velocity_mean` | `Differentiable[GridVectorField] | None` |  | Time-averaged (RANS) velocity field over the last steps // 2 steps of the rollout, same shape as result. Computed on solvers feasible for drag optimisation (xlb, phiflow, pict) when an obstacle and inflow profile are present. None on periodic/lid-cavity paths. |

:::

### XLB

**Scheme:** LBM KBC/BGK D3Q27 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** jax &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Reverse-mode AD &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float64 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** Yes

**Upstream docs:** [https://github.com/Autodesk/XLB](https://github.com/Autodesk/XLB)

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/navier-stokes-grid/xlb`

JAX-native lattice Boltzmann solver supporting D2Q9 and D3Q19 stencils on GPU. Gradients via source-transformation AD through the collision-streaming loop. Note: the LBM recovers incompressible Navier–Stokes only to O(Ma²) via Chapman–Enskog expansion, giving an intrinsic compressibility error floor at fine grids.

**Wrapping:** Pure JAX: XLB's JAX backend runs the LBM collision-streaming loop. VJP via ``jax.vjp`` through the full loop.

::: {.callout-warning}
#### Known limitations

- Intrinsic O(Ma²) compressibility error: the LBM recovers incompressible NS only approximately. At N=128, dt=0.01, Ma ≈ 0.2, giving ~4% error floor.
- BGK collision instability at low viscosity: relaxation time approaches the stability boundary as viscosity decreases.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `v0` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Initial velocity field, shape (N, N, 1, 2). Default: 64×64 divergence-free random vortex field (seed=42). |
| `viscosity` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Kinematic viscosity ν. |
| `dt` | `Array[(1,), Float32]` | ✓ | Timestep size. |
| `steps` | `int` |  | Number of timesteps. Total simulated time = steps × dt. |
| `domain_extent` | `float` |  | Side length of the square periodic domain. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `GridBC` |  | Per-face boundary conditions. Default: all periodic. |
| `obstacle` | `GridObstacle | None` |  | Optional solid obstacle embedded in the domain (no-slip walls). |
| `inflow_profile` | `Differentiable[Array[(None,), Float32]] | None` |  | 1-D inlet velocity profile u_x(y), shape (N,). When provided, overrides the x_lo Dirichlet BC with a spatially-varying inlet. u_y is set to zero. Used for inflow-profile optimisation (e.g. cylinder drag minimisation). |
| `lid_velocity` | `Differentiable[Array[(None, None, 2), Float32]] | None` |  | Lid velocity field for 3-D lid-driven cavity experiments, shape (N, N, 2). The two components are the x- and y-tangential velocities on the moving top lid. When provided the solver runs in non-periodic cavity mode with no-slip walls. Used as the optimisation variable in lid_cavity experiments. |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `result` | `GridVectorField` | ✓ | Final velocity field, same shape as v0. |
| `drag` | `Differentiable[Array[(1,), Float32]] | None` |  | x-direction drag force on the embedded obstacle, shape (1,). Computed as the surface integral of pressure and viscous stress: F_x = ∮_S (p n_x − μ (∂u/∂n)_x) dS. None when no obstacle is present. |
| `velocity_mean` | `Differentiable[GridVectorField] | None` |  | Time-averaged (RANS) velocity field over the last steps // 2 steps of the rollout, same shape as result. Computed on solvers feasible for drag optimisation (xlb, phiflow, pict) when an obstacle and inflow profile are present. None on periodic/lid-cavity paths. |

:::


## Structural Mechanics

### deal.II

**Scheme:** FEM Q1 linear elasticity (SIMP, analytic self-adjoint gradient) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** dealii &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Discrete adjoint &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float64 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** No

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/structural-mesh/dealii`

Solves structural problems with Q1 elements using the industry-grade C++ finite-element library deal.II. Gradients via hand-derived analytical adjoint sensitivities. Used as the reference solver for structural and thermal domains.

**Wrapping:** C++ subprocess: deal.II solver compiled as a shared library, called via ctypes. VJP via hand-derived analytical adjoint sensitivities in C++.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `rho` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell density field, shape (n_max_cells,). Active slice = rho[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. Values in [0, 1]: 0 = void, 1 = solid. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `MeshBC` |  | Mesh boundary conditions. dirichlet.mask/values prescribe displacement (values shape (n_groups, 3), None → zero); neumann.mask/values prescribe surface traction (values shape (n_groups, 3)). |
| `hex_mesh` | `HexMesh` |  | Hexahedral mesh (8-node hex elements). n_points / n_faces give the active slice sizes. |
| `E_max` | `float` |  | Young's modulus of the fully solid material [MPa]. |
| `nu` | `float` |  | Poisson's ratio. |
| `xmin` | `float` |  | Void stiffness ratio (E_min = xmin * E_max). |
| `penal` | `float` |  | SIMP penalisation exponent (E(ρ) = E_min + (E_max−E_min)·ρ^penal). |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `compliance` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Structural compliance C = F^T U (scalar). Minimising compliance is equivalent to maximising stiffness. |
| `von_mises_stress` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell von Mises stress at element centroids, shape (n_cells,). |
| `displacement` | `Array[(None, 3), Float32]` | ✓ | Nodal displacement field, shape (n_nodes, 3). |

:::

### FEniCS

**Scheme:** FEM CG1 linear elasticity (SIMP, dolfin-adjoint) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** fenics &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Discrete adjoint &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float64 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** No

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/structural-mesh/fenics`

Finite-element solver using P1 elements for structural problems. dolfin-adjoint automates the discrete adjoint by replaying the forward tape.

**Wrapping:** In-process Python: FEniCS/dolfin assembles and solves via PETSc. VJP via dolfin-adjoint tape replay (discrete adjoint).

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `rho` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell density field, shape (n_max_cells,). Active slice = rho[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. Values in [0, 1]: 0 = void, 1 = solid. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `MeshBC` |  | Mesh boundary conditions. dirichlet.mask/values prescribe displacement (values shape (n_groups, 3), None → zero); neumann.mask/values prescribe surface traction (values shape (n_groups, 3)). |
| `hex_mesh` | `HexMesh` |  | Hexahedral mesh (8-node hex elements). n_points / n_faces give the active slice sizes. |
| `E_max` | `float` |  | Young's modulus of the fully solid material. |
| `nu` | `float` |  | Poisson's ratio. |
| `xmin` | `float` |  | Void stiffness ratio (E_min = xmin * E_max). |
| `penal` | `float` |  | SIMP penalisation exponent (E(rho) = E_min + (E_max-E_min)*rho^penal). |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `compliance` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Structural compliance C = F^T U (scalar). Minimising compliance is equivalent to maximising stiffness. |
| `von_mises_stress` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell von Mises stress at element centroids, shape (n_cells,). |
| `displacement` | `Array[(None, 3), Float32]` | ✓ | Nodal displacement field, shape (n_nodes, 3). |

:::

### Firedrake

**Scheme:** FEM CG1 linear elasticity (SIMP, pyadjoint ReducedFunctional) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** firedrake &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Discrete adjoint &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float64 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** No

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/structural-mesh/firedrake`

Mirrors the FEniCS P1/CG1 formulation for structural problems. Differentiates via firedrake-adjoint, providing an independent tape-based adjoint implementation for cross-validation.

**Wrapping:** In-process Python: Firedrake assembles and solves via PETSc. VJP via firedrake-adjoint/pyadjoint tape replay (discrete adjoint).

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `rho` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell density field, shape (n_max_cells,). Active slice = rho[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. Values in [0, 1]: 0 = void, 1 = solid. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `MeshBC` |  | Mesh boundary conditions. dirichlet.mask/values prescribe displacement (values shape (n_groups, 3), None → zero); neumann.mask/values prescribe surface traction (values shape (n_groups, 3)). |
| `hex_mesh` | `HexMesh` |  | Hexahedral mesh (8-node hex elements). n_points / n_faces give the active slice sizes. |
| `E_max` | `float` |  | Young's modulus of the fully solid material. |
| `nu` | `float` |  | Poisson's ratio. |
| `xmin` | `float` |  | Void stiffness ratio (E_min = xmin * E_max). |
| `penal` | `float` |  | SIMP penalisation exponent (E(rho) = E_min + (E_max-E_min)*rho^penal). |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `compliance` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Structural compliance C = F^T U (scalar). Minimising compliance is equivalent to maximising stiffness. |
| `von_mises_stress` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell von Mises stress at element centroids, shape (n_cells,). |
| `displacement` | `Array[(None, 3), Float32]` | ✓ | Nodal displacement field, shape (n_nodes, 3). |

:::

### JAX-FEM

**Scheme:** FEM HEX8 linear elasticity (SIMP, adjoint AD) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** jax &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Hybrid (analytic rules + AD) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float32 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** Yes

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/structural-mesh/jax-fem`

Solves heat conduction and linear elasticity with trilinear HEX8 finite elements in JAX. Gradients via AD through the assembled system.

**Wrapping:** Pure JAX: JAX-FEM assembles and solves the FE system in JAX. VJP via ``jax.vjp`` through the assembled linear solve.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `rho` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell density field, shape (n_max_cells,). Active slice = rho[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. Values in [0, 1]: 0 = void, 1 = solid. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `MeshBC` |  | Mesh boundary conditions. dirichlet.mask/values prescribe displacement (values shape (n_groups, 3), None → zero); neumann.mask/values prescribe surface traction (values shape (n_groups, 3)). |
| `hex_mesh` | `HexMesh` |  | Hexahedral mesh (8-node hex elements). n_points / n_faces give the active slice sizes. |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `compliance` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Structural compliance C = F^T U (scalar). Minimising compliance is equivalent to maximising stiffness. |
| `von_mises_stress` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell von Mises stress at element centroids, shape (n_cells,). |
| `displacement` | `Array[(None, 3), Float32]` | ✓ | Nodal displacement field, shape (n_nodes, 3). |

:::

### TopOpt.jl

**Scheme:** FEM HEX8 linear elasticity (SIMP, analytical adjoint) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** julia &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Discrete adjoint &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float64 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** No

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/structural-mesh/topopt-jl`

SIMP topology optimization for linear elasticity with HEX8 elements in Julia, using analytical adjoint sensitivities.

**Wrapping:** Julia subprocess: the TopOpt solver is called via ``juliacall``. VJP via analytical adjoint sensitivities computed in Julia.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `rho` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell density field, shape (n_max_cells,). Active slice = rho[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. Values in [0, 1]: 0 = void, 1 = solid. |
| `boundary_conditions` | `MeshBC` |  | Mesh boundary conditions. dirichlet.mask/values prescribe displacement (values shape (n_groups, 3), None → zero); neumann.mask/values prescribe surface traction (values shape (n_groups, 3)). |
| `hex_mesh` | `HexMesh` |  | Hexahedral mesh (8-node hex elements). n_points / n_faces give the active slice sizes. |
| `E` | `float` |  | Young's modulus of the solid material. |
| `nu` | `float` |  | Poisson's ratio. |
| `xmin` | `float` |  | Minimum density (void stiffness) to prevent singular stiffness matrix. |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `compliance` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Structural compliance C = F^T U (scalar). Minimising compliance is equivalent to maximising stiffness. |
| `von_mises_stress` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell von Mises stress at element centroids, shape (n_cells,). |
| `displacement` | `Array[(None, 3), Float32]` | ✓ | Nodal displacement field, shape (n_nodes, 3). |

:::


## Heat Conduction

### deal.II

**Scheme:** FEM Q1 heat conduction (SIMP, analytic self-adjoint gradient) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** dealii &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Discrete adjoint &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float64 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** No

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/thermal-mesh/dealii-heat`

Solves thermal problems with Q1 elements using the industry-grade C++ finite-element library deal.II. Gradients via hand-derived analytical adjoint sensitivities. Used as the reference solver for the thermal domain.

**Wrapping:** C++ subprocess: deal.II solver compiled as a shared library, called via ctypes. VJP via hand-derived analytical adjoint sensitivities in C++.

::: {.callout-note}
#### Excluded experiments

- **recovery/conductivity_recovery**: identification_error_rho_vjp_not_implemented
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `rho` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell density field, shape (n_max_cells,). Active slice = rho[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. ρ=1 → fully conducting, ρ=0 → insulating. |
| `source` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-element volumetric heat source (W/m³), shape (n_cells,). Active slice = source[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. Zero by default (topology-optimisation mode; no body heat source). |
| `target_temperature` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` |  | Per-node target temperature for the inverse (source-identification) objective, shape (n_nodes,). identification_error = ||T - target_temperature||²_2. Zero by default (not used in topology-optimisation mode). |
| `boundary_conditions` | `MeshBC` |  | Mesh boundary conditions. dirichlet.mask/values prescribe temperature (values shape (n_groups, 1), None → zero); neumann.mask/values prescribe heat flux (values shape (n_groups, 1)). |
| `hex_mesh` | `HexMesh` |  | Hexahedral mesh (8-node hex elements). n_points / n_faces give the active slice sizes. |
| `k_max` | `float` |  | Maximum thermal conductivity (fully solid material). |
| `p_exp` | `float` |  | SIMP penalisation exponent p (k(ρ) = k_min + (k_max−k_min)·ρ^p). |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `thermal_compliance` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Total thermal compliance C = ∮_Γ_N q_n · T dΓ (scalar). Equals the work done by the Neumann heat flux on the temperature field. |
| `temperature` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Temperature field at mesh vertices/cells, shape (n_vertices,). |
| `identification_error` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Source-identification objective: ||T - target_temperature||²_2 (scalar). Computed as sum((T_nodes - target_temperature[:n_nodes])²). Zero when target_temperature is all-zeros (topology-optimisation mode). |

:::

### FEniCS

**Scheme:** FEM P1 heat conduction (SIMP, dolfin-adjoint) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** fenics &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Discrete adjoint &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float64 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** No

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/thermal-mesh/fenics-heat`

Finite-element solver using P1 elements for thermal problems. dolfin-adjoint automates the discrete adjoint by replaying the forward tape.

**Wrapping:** In-process Python: FEniCS/dolfin assembles and solves via PETSc. VJP via dolfin-adjoint tape replay (discrete adjoint).

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `rho` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell density field, shape (n_max_cells,). Active slice = rho[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. ρ=1 → fully conducting, ρ=0 → insulating. |
| `source` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-element volumetric heat source (W/m³), shape (n_cells,). Active slice = source[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. Zero by default (topology-optimisation mode; no body heat source). |
| `target_temperature` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` |  | Per-node target temperature for the inverse (source-identification) objective, shape (n_nodes,). identification_error = ||T - target_temperature||²_2. Zero by default (not used in topology-optimisation mode). |
| `boundary_conditions` | `MeshBC` |  | Mesh boundary conditions. dirichlet.mask/values prescribe temperature (values shape (n_groups, 1), None → zero); neumann.mask/values prescribe heat flux (values shape (n_groups, 1)). |
| `hex_mesh` | `HexMesh` |  | Hexahedral mesh (8-node hex elements). n_points / n_faces give the active slice sizes. |
| `k_max` | `float` |  | Maximum thermal conductivity (fully solid/conducting material). |
| `p_exp` | `float` |  | SIMP penalisation exponent p (k(ρ) = k_min + (k_max−k_min)·ρ^p). |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `thermal_compliance` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Total thermal compliance C = ∮_Γ_N q_n · T dΓ (scalar). Equals the work done by the Neumann heat flux on the temperature field. |
| `temperature` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Temperature field at mesh vertices/cells, shape (n_vertices,). |
| `identification_error` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Source-identification objective: ||T - target_temperature||²_2 (scalar). Computed as sum((T_nodes - target_temperature[:n_nodes])²). Zero when target_temperature is all-zeros (topology-optimisation mode). |

:::

### Firedrake

**Scheme:** FEM P1 heat conduction (SIMP, firedrake-adjoint) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** firedrake &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Discrete adjoint &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float64 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** No

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/thermal-mesh/firedrake-heat`

Mirrors the FEniCS P1/CG1 formulation for thermal problems. Differentiates via firedrake-adjoint, providing an independent tape-based adjoint implementation for cross-validation.

**Wrapping:** In-process Python: Firedrake assembles and solves via PETSc. VJP via firedrake-adjoint/pyadjoint tape replay (discrete adjoint).

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `rho` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell density field, shape (n_max_cells,). Active slice = rho[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. ρ=1 → fully conducting, ρ=0 → insulating. |
| `source` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-element volumetric heat source (W/m³), shape (n_cells,). Active slice = source[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. Zero by default (topology-optimisation mode; no body heat source). |
| `target_temperature` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` |  | Per-node target temperature for the inverse (source-identification) objective, shape (n_nodes,). identification_error = ||T - target_temperature||²_2. Zero by default (not used in topology-optimisation mode). |
| `boundary_conditions` | `MeshBC` |  | Mesh boundary conditions. dirichlet.mask/values prescribe temperature (values shape (n_groups, 1), None → zero); neumann.mask/values prescribe heat flux (values shape (n_groups, 1)). |
| `hex_mesh` | `HexMesh` |  | Hexahedral mesh (8-node hex elements). n_points / n_faces give the active slice sizes. |
| `k_max` | `float` |  | Maximum thermal conductivity (fully solid/conducting material). |
| `p_exp` | `float` |  | SIMP penalisation exponent p (k(ρ) = k_min + (k_max−k_min)·ρ^p). |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `thermal_compliance` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Total thermal compliance C = ∮_Γ_N q_n · T dΓ (scalar). Equals the work done by the Neumann heat flux on the temperature field. |
| `temperature` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Temperature field at mesh vertices/cells, shape (n_vertices,). |
| `identification_error` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Source-identification objective: ||T - target_temperature||²_2 (scalar). Computed as sum((T_nodes - target_temperature[:n_nodes])²). Zero when target_temperature is all-zeros (topology-optimisation mode). |

:::

### JAX-FEM

**Scheme:** FEM HEX8 heat conduction (SIMP, adjoint AD) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** jax &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Hybrid (analytic rules + AD) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float32 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** Yes

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/thermal-mesh/jax-fem`

Solves heat conduction and linear elasticity with trilinear HEX8 finite elements in JAX. Gradients via AD through the assembled system.

**Wrapping:** Pure JAX: JAX-FEM assembles and solves the FE system in JAX. VJP via ``jax.vjp`` through the assembled linear solve.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `rho` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell density field, shape (n_max_cells,). Active slice = rho[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. ρ=1 → fully conducting, ρ=0 → insulating. |
| `source` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-element volumetric heat source (W/m³), shape (n_cells,). Active slice = source[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. Zero by default (topology-optimisation mode; no body heat source). |
| `target_temperature` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` |  | Per-node target temperature for the inverse (source-identification) objective, shape (n_nodes,). identification_error = ||T - target_temperature||²_2. Zero by default (not used in topology-optimisation mode). |
| `boundary_conditions` | `MeshBC` |  | Mesh boundary conditions. dirichlet.mask/values prescribe temperature (values shape (n_groups, 1), None → zero); neumann.mask/values prescribe heat flux (values shape (n_groups, 1)). |
| `hex_mesh` | `HexMesh` |  | Hexahedral mesh (8-node hex elements). n_points / n_faces give the active slice sizes. |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `thermal_compliance` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Total thermal compliance C = ∮_Γ_N q_n · T dΓ (scalar). Equals the work done by the Neumann heat flux on the temperature field. |
| `temperature` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Temperature field at mesh vertices/cells, shape (n_vertices,). |
| `identification_error` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Source-identification objective: ||T - target_temperature||²_2 (scalar). Computed as sum((T_nodes - target_temperature[:n_nodes])²). Zero when target_temperature is all-zeros (topology-optimisation mode). |

:::

### torch-fem

**Scheme:** FEM HEX8 heat conduction (SIMP, PyTorch autograd adjoint) &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Language:** pytorch &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Gradients:** Reverse-mode AD &nbsp;·&nbsp; **Precision:** float64 &nbsp;·&nbsp; **GPU:** Yes

**Path:** `mosaic/tesseracts/thermal-mesh/torch-fem`

Solves heat conduction with linear finite elements in PyTorch. Gradients via torch.autograd through the assembled system.

**Wrapping:** PyTorch: linear FE assembly and solve in pure PyTorch. VJP via ``torch.autograd`` through the assembled system.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
#### Inputs / Outputs

**Inputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `rho` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-cell density field, shape (n_max_cells,). Active slice = rho[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. ρ=1 → fully conducting, ρ=0 → insulating. |
| `source` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Per-element volumetric heat source (W/m³), shape (n_cells,). Active slice = source[:hex_mesh.n_faces]. Zero by default (topology-optimisation mode; no body heat source). |
| `target_temperature` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` |  | Per-node target temperature for the inverse (source-identification) objective, shape (n_nodes,). identification_error = ||T - target_temperature||²_2. Zero by default (not used in topology-optimisation mode). |
| `boundary_conditions` | `MeshBC` |  | Mesh boundary conditions. dirichlet.mask/values prescribe temperature (values shape (n_groups, 1), None → zero); neumann.mask/values prescribe heat flux (values shape (n_groups, 1)). |
| `hex_mesh` | `HexMesh` |  | Hexahedral mesh (8-node hex elements). n_points / n_faces give the active slice sizes. |
| `k_max` | `float` |  | Maximum thermal conductivity (fully solid material). |
| `p_exp` | `float` |  | SIMP penalisation exponent p (k(ρ) = k_min + (k_max−k_min)·ρ^p). |

**Outputs**

| Field | Type | ∂ | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `thermal_compliance` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Total thermal compliance C = ∮_Γ_N q_n · T dΓ (scalar). Equals the work done by the Neumann heat flux on the temperature field. |
| `temperature` | `Array[(None,), Float32]` | ✓ | Temperature field at mesh vertices/cells, shape (n_vertices,). |
| `identification_error` | `Array[(), Float32]` | ✓ | Source-identification objective: ||T - target_temperature||²_2 (scalar). Computed as sum((T_nodes - target_temperature[:n_nodes])²). Zero when target_temperature is all-zeros (topology-optimisation mode). |

:::
